title: scenario_botnet_infection
description: Corporate endpoints are enrolled in a botnet and used for DDoS attacks and spam campaigns, implicating the organization in malicious activity.

timeline.title: ISP Abuse Notification
timeline.description: ISP contacts the organization reporting that outbound traffic from the corporate IP block is participating in a DDoS attack against a third-party target.

timeline.title: Internal Traffic Analysis
timeline.description: Network team confirms high-volume SYN-flood traffic originating from multiple internal workstations to external targets.

timeline.title: Botnet C2 Beaconing Found
timeline.description: Affected machines are beaconing to a botnet command-and-control infrastructure. 28 machines identified across 3 office locations.

timeline.title: Spam Campaign Detected
timeline.description: Email gateway logs reveal botnet is also using corporate mail servers for outbound spam. Domain reputation being damaged.

timeline.title: Infection Vector Identified
timeline.description: Initial compromise traced to a malicious browser extension downloaded from an unofficial extension store.

timeline.title: Eradication Underway
timeline.description: Affected endpoints quarantined and cleaned. Browser extension pushed to removal via MDM. C2 domains blocked.

timeline.title: Reputation Remediation
timeline.description: IP block delisting requests submitted to ISP and major anti-spam blacklists. Email deliverability restored.

node-1.title: ISP Reports Corporate IPs Participating in DDoS
node-1.situation: Your ISP sends an abuse notification: traffic from your corporate IP range is being used in a large-scale DDoS attack against a financial services firm. If not resolved quickly, your IP block may be null-routed. Initial network analysis confirms the traffic is real.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Identify the specific internal source IPs generating the traffic, segment them off the network, and begin correlating with endpoint telemetry.

options.label: B
options.text: Null-route all outbound traffic to stop the attack — this will halt business operations.

options.label: C
options.text: Dispute the ISP's claim and wait for more evidence before taking any action.

node-2.title: 28 Infected Machines Identified
node-2.situation: EDR telemetry and network logs confirm 28 endpoints across 3 office locations are infected and receiving commands from a botnet C2 infrastructure. They are also being used for outbound spam, damaging your email domain reputation.
node-2.question: How do you contain the outbreak across multiple sites?

options.label: A
options.text: Push firewall rules to block C2 communication, quarantine all 28 endpoints via MDM, and notify site managers at each location.

options.label: B
options.text: Clean machines at headquarters first and address remote sites next week.

node-3.title: Infection Vector: Malicious Browser Extension
node-3.situation: Root cause analysis identifies the initial infection vector as a malicious browser extension mimicking a popular productivity tool, distributed via an unofficial extension store. Multiple employees installed it after seeing it promoted on social media.
node-3.question: How do you remediate the browser extension and prevent reinstallation?

options.label: A
options.text: Use MDM to force-remove the extension from all corporate devices, restrict browser extension installation to an approved allowlist, and notify all users.

options.label: B
options.text: Advise users to uninstall the extension themselves and send an awareness email.

node-4.title: Domain Reputation and Legal Exposure
node-4.situation: The organization's mail domain has been added to three major anti-spam blacklists. Email to customers is being blocked. Additionally, legal is concerned about liability for the DDoS attack against the financial services firm conducted via your infrastructure.
node-4.question: How do you address reputation damage and legal exposure?

options.label: A
options.text: Submit delisting requests to all blacklists with evidence of remediation, contact the targeted firm to provide incident details, and file a report with law enforcement documenting the botnet operators.

options.label: B
options.text: Change the organization's outbound IP addresses to bypass the blacklists without addressing the root cause.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: All infected endpoints cleaned and verified. C2 communication blocked enterprise-wide. Browser extension allowlisting enforced. Email domain delisted and reputation restored. Law enforcement briefed on botnet infrastructure IOCs.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
